title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Superintendent Weston questions Kenneth Marshall regarding his wife Eleanor's activities and her relationship with Patrick Redfern. Marshall claims he found Eleanor's room empty after breakfast and subsequently searched for her on the beach. When Weston notes that other hotel guests observed Eleanor and Redfern becoming inseparable, Marshall dismisses the observation as gossip, stating he does not interfere with his wife's habits. Hercule Poirot observes that understanding Eleanor's personality is essential to identifying her killer, noting that Marshall has provided very little information regarding her character.

Providing an account of his morning, Marshall states that he returned to the hotel at approximately 10:40 after swimming. He claims he spent the hour from 10:50 to 11:50 in his room writing emails. To support this alibi, he offers three unstamped letters containing financial documents and lists, suggesting that a nearby maid might have heard the sound of his typewriter. He further notes that at 12:00, he played tennis with Mrs. Redfern, Miss Darnley, and Mr. Gardner.

When Weston inquires about Eleanor's inheritance, Marshall reveals that Eleanor had no close relatives and had not written a will, which would leave her entire estate to him. He explains that Eleanor became a wealthy woman after receiving a bequest of approximately £50,000 from her late friend, Sir Roger Erskine. This revelation prompts Inspector Corrigan to question Marshall's assertion regarding the lack of a will.
